name: James Reese Europe
description: American jazz musician and United States Army officer (1880-1919)
abstract: James Reese Europe (February 22, 1880 – May 9, 1919) was an American ragtime and early jazz bandleader, arranger, and composer. A leading figure in New York City's African-American music scene during the 1910s, he was among the first African-American musicians to achieve mainstream recognition.

works_created.t: All of No Man’s Land Is Ours
On Patrol in No Man's Land

about_quotes.speaker: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve got to go back to Eubie. Because Eubie wrote a song during World War I... And he went to Paris with James Reese Europe in 1919 or 1920 I believe.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 21, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: Whereas, when Jim Europe… James Europe. He went to Europe, went to Paris with this military band...

about_quotes.speaker: Vincent Pelote
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve heard people say, “Wasn’t James Reese Europe the first person to put jazz on record?”

about_quotes.speaker: Catherine Russell
about_quotes.evidence: And your grandfather played with James Reese, is that right? ... James Reese Europe. Yeah.
